calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2025 Set For Trial On Feb-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant Richard N. Denning failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set Two, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (Id. section 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).) Wells Fargo does not seek sanctions, but Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.280, subdivision (c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by Denning to Wells Fargo within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
